items.title: Security requirements are defined and tracked
items.description: Functional and non-functional security requirements (authN/authZ, data classification, regulatory obligations) are captured as first-class backlog items, not assumed. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm security requirements exist in the issue tracker, are linked to user stories, and have acceptance criteria reviewed before development starts. Common tags: requirements, sdlc, governance.
items.how_to_find: Confirm security requirements exist in the issue tracker, are linked to user stories, and have acceptance criteria reviewed before development starts.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Security requirements are defined and tracked" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Shift-Left Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Threat modeling is performed during design
items.description: New features and architectural changes undergo structured threat modeling (e.g., STRIDE) to identify trust boundaries, attack surfaces, and mitigations before code is written. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify threat models exist for major components, are updated on significant design changes, and produce tracked mitigation items. Common tags: threat-modeling, design, sdlc.
items.how_to_find: Verify threat models exist for major components, are updated on significant design changes, and produce tracked mitigation items.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Threat modeling is performed during design" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Shift-Left Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Security gates block insecure promotions
items.description: CI/CD pipelines enforce policy gates (failing builds on critical findings, required reviews, signed artifacts) that prevent code from advancing without meeting a security bar. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm pipeline configuration fails on critical/high findings, that bypass requires documented approval, and that gates cannot be silently disabled. Common tags: ci-cd, gates, pipeline.
items.how_to_find: Confirm pipeline configuration fails on critical/high findings, that bypass requires documented approval, and that gates cannot be silently disabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Security gates block insecure promotions" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Shift-Left Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Security design reviews are mandatory for high-risk changes
items.description: High-blast-radius changes (auth, crypto, payment, multi-tenant boundaries) require a documented security design review before implementation proceeds. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a trigger list exists for mandatory reviews and that recent high-risk PRs show evidence of completed security design review. Common tags: design, review, governance.
items.how_to_find: Verify a trigger list exists for mandatory reviews and that recent high-risk PRs show evidence of completed security design review.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Security design reviews are mandatory for high-risk changes" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Shift-Left Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Secure SDLC Integration — Developer Enablement & Training

items.title: Secure coding training is delivered and tracked
items.description: Developers receive role-relevant secure coding training (OWASP Top 10, language-specific pitfalls) with completion tracked and refreshed periodically. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm training records exist, cover relevant languages/frameworks, and are refreshed at least annually with measurable completion rates. Common tags: training, education, culture.
items.how_to_find: Confirm training records exist, cover relevant languages/frameworks, and are refreshed at least annually with measurable completion rates.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Secure coding training is delivered and tracked" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Developer Enablement & Training. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: A security champions program exists
items.description: Each delivery team has an embedded security champion who reviews designs, triages findings, and acts as the local liaison to the central security team. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify champions are named per team, have defined responsibilities and time allocation, and meet regularly with the security function. Common tags: champions, culture, governance.
items.how_to_find: Verify champions are named per team, have defined responsibilities and time allocation, and meet regularly with the security function.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "A security champions program exists" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Developer Enablement & Training. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Secure coding standards and golden paths are published
items.description: The organization publishes secure coding standards, hardened libraries, and paved-road templates so developers default to safe patterns. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm coding standards exist, reference approved crypto/auth libraries, and that paved-road scaffolds embed security controls by default. Common tags: standards, golden-path, guidance.
items.how_to_find: Confirm coding standards exist, reference approved crypto/auth libraries, and that paved-road scaffolds embed security controls by default.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Secure coding standards and golden paths are published" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Developer Enablement & Training. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Security findings drive targeted education
items.description: Recurring vulnerability classes from scanning and pentests feed back into training content and guidance, closing the knowledge gap that produced them. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify there is a mechanism linking top recurring finding categories to updated guidance, lunch-and-learns, or remediation playbooks. Common tags: feedback, training, metrics.
items.how_to_find: Verify there is a mechanism linking top recurring finding categories to updated guidance, lunch-and-learns, or remediation playbooks.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Security findings drive targeted education" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secure SDLC Integration — Developer Enablement & Training. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Automated Security Testing — Static Application Security Testing (SAST)

items.title: SAST runs automatically in the pipeline
items.description: Static analysis executes on every pull request and merge, scanning all in-scope repositories rather than running only ad hoc. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm SAST is wired into CI for all primary repos, runs on PRs, and posts results back to the developer in-context. Common tags: sast, pipeline, ci-cd.
items.how_to_find: Confirm SAST is wired into CI for all primary repos, runs on PRs, and posts results back to the developer in-context.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "SAST runs automatically in the pipeline" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Static Application Security Testing (SAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: A SAST baseline separates new from legacy findings
items.description: A committed baseline allows the pipeline to fail only on newly introduced issues, avoiding alert fatigue from a legacy backlog while still tracking it. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a baseline file exists, is reviewed periodically, and that net-new critical findings break the build while legacy debt is tracked separately. Common tags: sast, baseline, triage.
items.how_to_find: Verify a baseline file exists, is reviewed periodically, and that net-new critical findings break the build while legacy debt is tracked separately.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "A SAST baseline separates new from legacy findings" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Static Application Security Testing (SAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: False positives are managed with auditable suppressions
items.description: Suppressions and rule tuning are version-controlled, justified, and reviewed, so the tool stays trustworthy and suppression is not abused to hide real risk. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm suppressions live in code with documented rationale, expire or are re-reviewed, and that suppression rates are monitored. Common tags: sast, false-positives, triage.
items.how_to_find: Confirm suppressions live in code with documented rationale, expire or are re-reviewed, and that suppression rates are monitored.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "False positives are managed with auditable suppressions" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Static Application Security Testing (SAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: SAST ruleset coverage matches the tech stack
items.description: Enabled rulesets cover every primary language and framework in use, including security-relevant categories like injection, deserialization, and SSRF. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify enabled rule packs map to all languages/frameworks in the codebase and that high-impact categories are not disabled. Common tags: sast, coverage, configuration.
items.how_to_find: Verify enabled rule packs map to all languages/frameworks in the codebase and that high-impact categories are not disabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "SAST ruleset coverage matches the tech stack" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Static Application Security Testing (SAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Automated Security Testing — Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST)

items.title: DAST scans run automatically against deployed builds
items.description: Dynamic scans execute against a staging/test deployment as part of the pipeline, catching runtime issues SAST cannot see. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm DAST runs against a representative environment per release candidate and that results are routed into the tracking workflow. Common tags: dast, pipeline, runtime.
items.how_to_find: Confirm DAST runs against a representative environment per release candidate and that results are routed into the tracking workflow.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "DAST scans run automatically against deployed builds" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Authenticated and API scanning is configured
items.description: DAST is configured with valid sessions and API specifications so it reaches authenticated surfaces and exercises real endpoints, not just public pages. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify the scanner has working auth (session/token) and is fed OpenAPI/GraphQL schemas to maximize coverage of protected functionality. Common tags: dast, authentication, api.
items.how_to_find: Verify the scanner has working auth (session/token) and is fed OpenAPI/GraphQL schemas to maximize coverage of protected functionality.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Authenticated and API scanning is configured" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: DAST runs against safe, non-production targets
items.description: Active scanning is pointed at dedicated test environments with seeded data to avoid disrupting production or corrupting live data. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm scan targets are non-production, that destructive checks are constrained, and that production scanning (if any) is explicitly authorized and passive. Common tags: dast, scope, safety.
items.how_to_find: Confirm scan targets are non-production, that destructive checks are constrained, and that production scanning (if any) is explicitly authorized and passive.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "DAST runs against safe, non-production targets" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: DAST findings are deduplicated and triaged
items.description: Results are correlated across scans to reduce noise, and confirmed issues are tracked to remediation with severity-based SLAs. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify findings flow into a vulnerability management tool, duplicates are merged, and confirmed issues have owners and due dates. Common tags: dast, triage, metrics.
items.how_to_find: Verify findings flow into a vulnerability management tool, duplicates are merged, and confirmed issues have owners and due dates.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "DAST findings are deduplicated and triaged" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Automated Security Testing — Software Composition Analysis (SCA)

items.title: Dependencies are scanned for known vulnerabilities
items.description: Every build scans direct and transitive dependencies against vulnerability databases and fails or flags on critical CVEs. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm SCA runs on all repos including transitive dependencies, and that critical/high CVEs are surfaced with fix versions. Common tags: sca, dependencies, cve.
items.how_to_find: Confirm SCA runs on all repos including transitive dependencies, and that critical/high CVEs are surfaced with fix versions.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Dependencies are scanned for known vulnerabilities" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Software Composition Analysis (SCA). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: OWASP Top Ten — Vulnerable and Outdated Components

items.title: License compliance is enforced
items.description: SCA evaluates dependency licenses against an allow/deny policy to prevent introduction of incompatible or restrictive licenses. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a license policy exists, that builds flag disallowed licenses, and that exceptions are documented and approved. Common tags: sca, license, compliance.
items.how_to_find: Verify a license policy exists, that builds flag disallowed licenses, and that exceptions are documented and approved.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "License compliance is enforced" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Software Composition Analysis (SCA). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: SCA prioritizes reachable, exploitable vulnerabilities
items.description: Findings are prioritized by exploitability (reachability, EPSS, KEV) so teams remediate truly risky issues first rather than chasing every CVE. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm prioritization uses reachability and/or CISA KEV/EPSS signals, and that remediation effort tracks against exploitable issues. Common tags: sca, prioritization, cve.
items.how_to_find: Confirm prioritization uses reachability and/or CISA KEV/EPSS signals, and that remediation effort tracks against exploitable issues.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "SCA prioritizes reachable, exploitable vulnerabilities" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Software Composition Analysis (SCA). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: OWASP Top Ten — Vulnerable and Outdated Components

items.title: Automated dependency updates are enabled
items.description: Automated update tooling proposes upgrades for vulnerable and outdated dependencies, keeping the dependency surface current with minimal toil. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify automated update PRs are enabled across repos, are reviewed and merged regularly, and that stale critical updates are not ignored. Common tags: sca, patching, automation.
items.how_to_find: Verify automated update PRs are enabled across repos, are reviewed and merged regularly, and that stale critical updates are not ignored.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Automated dependency updates are enabled" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Software Composition Analysis (SCA). Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Pre-commit secret scanning is in place
items.description: Developer machines run pre-commit hooks that block credentials, keys, and tokens before they ever reach the remote repository. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm pre-commit secret hooks are installed across teams and that they block (not just warn) on detected secrets. Common tags: secrets, pre-commit, prevention.
items.how_to_find: Confirm pre-commit secret hooks are installed across teams and that they block (not just warn) on detected secrets.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Pre-commit secret scanning is in place" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Secret Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Pipeline secret scanning enforces a backstop
items.description: CI scans every push and PR for secrets as a server-side backstop that does not depend on developers enabling local hooks. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify CI secret scanning runs on all repos and pushes, fails the build on verified secrets, and cannot be skipped without approval. Common tags: secrets, pipeline, ci-cd.
items.how_to_find: Verify CI secret scanning runs on all repos and pushes, fails the build on verified secrets, and cannot be skipped without approval.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Pipeline secret scanning enforces a backstop" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Secret Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Repository history is scanned for leaked secrets
items.description: Full git history is periodically scanned because secrets removed from HEAD may still be retrievable in prior commits. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm historical scans run on all repos and that any discovered secrets are treated as compromised and rotated. Common tags: secrets, history, detection.
items.how_to_find: Confirm historical scans run on all repos and that any discovered secrets are treated as compromised and rotated.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Repository history is scanned for leaked secrets" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Secret Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Detected secrets trigger immediate rotation
items.description: A defined process treats any exposed secret as compromised: revoke, rotate, and review for abuse, regardless of repository visibility. If exploited, this often leads to full account or environment compromise and must be treated as a release blocker. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a documented rotation runbook exists, has measurable response times, and that recent detections show evidence of rotation. Common tags: secrets, rotation, incident-response.
items.how_to_find: Verify a documented rotation runbook exists, has measurable response times, and that recent detections show evidence of rotation.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Detected secrets trigger immediate rotation" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Automated Security Testing — Secret Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Secrets are stored in a centralized secrets manager
items.description: Application and infrastructure secrets live in a dedicated secrets manager with access control and audit logging, not in source, env files, or wikis. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm a central secrets store is the source of truth, access is least-privilege, and all access is audit-logged. Common tags: secrets, vault, access-control.
items.how_to_find: Confirm a central secrets store is the source of truth, access is least-privilege, and all access is audit-logged.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Secrets are stored in a centralized secrets manager" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Centralized Secrets Store. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Dynamic, short-lived secrets are used where possible
items.description: Databases, cloud APIs, and services receive dynamically generated, short-TTL credentials instead of long-lived static keys. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify dynamic secret engines or short-lived tokens are used for high-value backends and that TTLs are minimized. Common tags: secrets, dynamic, least-privilege.
items.how_to_find: Verify dynamic secret engines or short-lived tokens are used for high-value backends and that TTLs are minimized.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Dynamic, short-lived secrets are used where possible" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Centralized Secrets Store. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Static secrets are rotated automatically
items.description: Any unavoidable long-lived secrets are rotated on a defined schedule and on suspected compromise, with rotation automated to avoid drift. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm rotation schedules exist, rotation is automated for supported secrets, and last-rotated timestamps are monitored. Common tags: secrets, rotation, automation.
items.how_to_find: Confirm rotation schedules exist, rotation is automated for supported secrets, and last-rotated timestamps are monitored.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Static secrets are rotated automatically" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Centralized Secrets Store. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Workloads authenticate via identity, not embedded keys
items.description: Services retrieve secrets using platform workload identity (e.g., cloud IAM roles, Kubernetes service accounts) rather than bootstrapping with embedded credentials. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify workloads use identity-based auth to the secrets store and that no bootstrap secrets are baked into images or manifests. Common tags: secrets, workload-identity, access-control.
items.how_to_find: Verify workloads use identity-based auth to the secrets store and that no bootstrap secrets are baked into images or manifests.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Workloads authenticate via identity, not embedded keys" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Centralized Secrets Store. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Pipeline secrets are masked and stored securely
items.description: CI secrets are stored as protected/masked variables or pulled from the secrets manager at runtime, never committed to the repository. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm CI variables holding secrets are marked masked/protected and that no secrets are present in pipeline definition files. Common tags: secrets, pipeline, ci-cd.
items.how_to_find: Confirm CI variables holding secrets are marked masked/protected and that no secrets are present in pipeline definition files.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Pipeline secrets are masked and stored securely" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Pipeline Secret Hygiene. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Secrets never appear in build logs or artifacts
items.description: Logging is configured to redact secrets and pipelines avoid echoing sensitive values; artifacts are inspected to ensure no credentials are baked in. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify masking is active in logs, that debug modes do not leak secrets, and spot-check artifacts/images for embedded credentials. Common tags: secrets, logging, leakage.
items.how_to_find: Verify masking is active in logs, that debug modes do not leak secrets, and spot-check artifacts/images for embedded credentials.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Secrets never appear in build logs or artifacts" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Pipeline Secret Hygiene. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Pipeline secrets are scoped and least-privilege
items.description: Secrets are scoped to specific environments, branches, and jobs so a compromised pipeline cannot reach production credentials it does not need. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm environment-scoped secrets, protected branches/environments, and that fork or PR pipelines cannot access production secrets. Common tags: secrets, least-privilege, pipeline.
items.how_to_find: Confirm environment-scoped secrets, protected branches/environments, and that fork or PR pipelines cannot access production secrets.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Pipeline secrets are scoped and least-privilege" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Pipeline Secret Hygiene. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Cloud access uses OIDC federation, not static keys
items.description: Pipelines authenticate to cloud providers via short-lived OIDC-federated tokens rather than long-lived static cloud access keys stored as CI secrets. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify CI-to-cloud auth uses OIDC federation with scoped trust policies, and that long-lived cloud keys are not stored in the pipeline. Common tags: secrets, oidc, cloud.
items.how_to_find: Verify CI-to-cloud auth uses OIDC federation with scoped trust policies, and that long-lived cloud keys are not stored in the pipeline.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Cloud access uses OIDC federation, not static keys" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Secrets Management — Pipeline Secret Hygiene. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Dependencies are pinned with committed lockfiles
items.description: Lockfiles pin exact versions and integrity hashes for all dependencies so builds are reproducible and tampering is detectable. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm lockfiles are committed for every project, builds install from the lockfile, and CI fails if the lockfile is out of sync. Common tags: supply-chain, pinning, lockfile.
items.how_to_find: Confirm lockfiles are committed for every project, builds install from the lockfile, and CI fails if the lockfile is out of sync.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Dependencies are pinned with committed lockfiles" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Dependency Integrity. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: SLSA — Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts
OWASP Top Ten — Software and Data Integrity Failures

items.title: Dependency confusion is mitigated
items.description: Private package scopes and registry configuration prevent attackers from publishing a malicious public package that shadows an internal name. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify internal packages use reserved scopes/namespaces, registries are explicitly configured, and public fallback for private names is disabled. Common tags: supply-chain, dependency-confusion, registry.
items.how_to_find: Verify internal packages use reserved scopes/namespaces, registries are explicitly configured, and public fallback for private names is disabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Dependency confusion is mitigated" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Dependency Integrity. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: CNCF Software Supply Chain Best Practices

items.title: Dependencies are pulled through a trusted proxy
items.description: An internal artifact proxy/registry caches and vets external packages, providing a control point for blocking and integrity verification. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm builds resolve dependencies through an internal proxy, that direct public-registry access is restricted, and that the proxy enforces policy. Common tags: supply-chain, registry, proxy.
items.how_to_find: Confirm builds resolve dependencies through an internal proxy, that direct public-registry access is restricted, and that the proxy enforces policy.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Dependencies are pulled through a trusted proxy" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Dependency Integrity. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: CNCF Software Supply Chain Best Practices
SLSA — Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts

items.title: Dependency integrity is verified at install time
items.description: Package manager integrity checks (hash/signature verification) are enabled so corrupted or tampered packages fail the build. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify integrity/hash verification is enforced during install and that signature verification is used where the ecosystem supports it. Common tags: supply-chain, integrity, verification.
items.how_to_find: Verify integrity/hash verification is enforced during install and that signature verification is used where the ecosystem supports it.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Dependency integrity is verified at install time" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Dependency Integrity. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: SLSA — Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts
OWASP Top Ten — Software and Data Integrity Failures

sections.title: Supply Chain Security — Artifact Signing & Provenance

items.title: Build artifacts are cryptographically signed
items.description: Containers and release artifacts are signed so consumers can verify authenticity and detect tampering before deployment. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm artifacts are signed in the pipeline, signatures are stored alongside artifacts, and deploy-time verification is enabled. Common tags: supply-chain, signing, provenance.
items.how_to_find: Confirm artifacts are signed in the pipeline, signatures are stored alongside artifacts, and deploy-time verification is enabled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Build artifacts are cryptographically signed" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Artifact Signing & Provenance. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: SLSA — Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts

items.title: SBOMs are generated and published for releases
items.description: A software bill of materials is produced for each artifact, enabling rapid impact analysis when a new dependency CVE is disclosed. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify SBOMs are generated per build in a standard format (SPDX/CycloneDX), stored as attestations, and queryable for incident response. Common tags: supply-chain, sbom, transparency.
items.how_to_find: Verify SBOMs are generated per build in a standard format (SPDX/CycloneDX), stored as attestations, and queryable for incident response.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "SBOMs are generated and published for releases" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Artifact Signing & Provenance. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Build provenance attestations are produced
items.description: The pipeline emits SLSA provenance describing how, where, and from what sources an artifact was built, enabling verification of build integrity. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm provenance attestations are generated for releases, reference the source commit and builder, and are verified before deployment. Common tags: supply-chain, provenance, slsa.
items.how_to_find: Confirm provenance attestations are generated for releases, reference the source commit and builder, and are verified before deployment.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Build provenance attestations are produced" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Artifact Signing & Provenance. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Signature and provenance are verified before deploy
items.description: Admission controls reject unsigned artifacts or those without valid provenance, closing the loop between signing and enforcement. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify deploy/admission policies require valid signatures and provenance, and that verification failures block rollout. Common tags: supply-chain, verification, policy.
items.how_to_find: Verify deploy/admission policies require valid signatures and provenance, and that verification failures block rollout.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Signature and provenance are verified before deploy" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Artifact Signing & Provenance. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: SLSA — Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts

sections.title: Supply Chain Security — Base Image Security

items.title: Base images come from trusted, pinned sources
items.description: Base images are sourced from a vetted internal registry or trusted publisher and pinned by digest, not floating tags like latest. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm base images are pinned by digest, sourced from an approved registry, and that use of arbitrary public images is restricted. Common tags: containers, base-image, supply-chain.
items.how_to_find: Confirm base images are pinned by digest, sourced from an approved registry, and that use of arbitrary public images is restricted.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Base images come from trusted, pinned sources" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Base Image Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Minimal/distroless base images are used
items.description: Images are kept minimal (distroless or slim) to reduce attack surface, remove shells and package managers, and shrink the vulnerability footprint. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify production images are minimal, do not ship unnecessary tooling, and run as a non-root user where feasible. Common tags: containers, hardening, attack-surface.
items.how_to_find: Verify production images are minimal, do not ship unnecessary tooling, and run as a non-root user where feasible.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Minimal/distroless base images are used" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Base Image Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Container images are scanned before and after publish
items.description: Images are scanned for OS and library vulnerabilities at build time and continuously in the registry as new CVEs are disclosed. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm image scanning runs in the build, that the registry rescans published images, and that critical findings block promotion. Common tags: containers, scanning, cve.
items.how_to_find: Confirm image scanning runs in the build, that the registry rescans published images, and that critical findings block promotion.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Container images are scanned before and after publish" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Base Image Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Base images are rebuilt and patched regularly
items.description: A defined cadence rebuilds images on updated bases so OS-level patches reach running workloads instead of lingering in stale layers. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a rebuild/patch cadence exists, that stale images are flagged, and that patched bases propagate to downstream images. Common tags: containers, patching, maintenance.
items.how_to_find: Verify a rebuild/patch cadence exists, that stale images are flagged, and that patched bases propagate to downstream images.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Base images are rebuilt and patched regularly" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Supply Chain Security — Base Image Security. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Infrastructure as Code — IaC Misconfiguration Scanning

items.title: IaC is scanned for misconfigurations in CI
items.description: Terraform, CloudFormation, and Kubernetes manifests are scanned on every change for insecure defaults before infrastructure is provisioned. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm IaC scanning runs on all infra repos in CI, covers the IaC formats in use, and fails on high-severity misconfigurations. Common tags: iac, scanning, ci-cd.
items.how_to_find: Confirm IaC scanning runs on all infra repos in CI, covers the IaC formats in use, and fails on high-severity misconfigurations.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "IaC is scanned for misconfigurations in CI" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — IaC Misconfiguration Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Kubernetes manifests are checked against hardening guidance
items.description: K8s manifests and Helm charts are scanned for insecure settings (privileged containers, host mounts, missing limits) against CIS Kubernetes guidance. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify manifest scanning flags privileged/hostPath/root settings and that critical issues are remediated before apply. Common tags: iac, kubernetes, hardening.
items.how_to_find: Verify manifest scanning flags privileged/hostPath/root settings and that critical issues are remediated before apply.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Kubernetes manifests are checked against hardening guidance" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — IaC Misconfiguration Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: IaC is validated against cloud security baselines
items.description: Scanning rules map to cloud provider security baselines (encryption at rest, public exposure, logging) so provisioned resources meet a known bar. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm rule sets align to CIS cloud benchmarks, cover encryption and public-access checks, and exceptions are documented. Common tags: iac, cloud, baseline.
items.how_to_find: Confirm rule sets align to CIS cloud benchmarks, cover encryption and public-access checks, and exceptions are documented.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "IaC is validated against cloud security baselines" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — IaC Misconfiguration Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: IaC scan results are tracked and not bypassed
items.description: Findings flow into tracking with owners, and suppressions are explicit and auditable rather than silently disabling rules. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify findings are tracked to remediation, that rule suppressions are justified in code, and bypass requires approval. Common tags: iac, triage, governance.
items.how_to_find: Verify findings are tracked to remediation, that rule suppressions are justified in code, and bypass requires approval.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "IaC scan results are tracked and not bypassed" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — IaC Misconfiguration Scanning. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Infrastructure as Code — Policy as Code

items.title: Security guardrails are codified as policy
items.description: Organizational security policies (no public buckets, required tags, mandatory encryption) are expressed as code and evaluated automatically. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm critical guardrails exist as policy, are version-controlled, and are evaluated in CI against IaC plans. Common tags: policy-as-code, guardrails, governance.
items.how_to_find: Confirm critical guardrails exist as policy, are version-controlled, and are evaluated in CI against IaC plans.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Security guardrails are codified as policy" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — Policy as Code. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Admission control enforces policy at deploy time
items.description: A policy engine in the cluster (or cloud) rejects non-compliant resources at admission, providing a runtime backstop beyond pipeline checks. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify admission policies are enforced (not audit-only) for critical controls and that violations are denied and logged. Common tags: policy-as-code, admission-control, kubernetes.
items.how_to_find: Verify admission policies are enforced (not audit-only) for critical controls and that violations are denied and logged.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Admission control enforces policy at deploy time" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — Policy as Code. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Policies are unit-tested and reviewed
items.description: Policy code has its own tests and change review so guardrails behave as intended and cannot be weakened without scrutiny. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm policies have test cases, changes go through review, and policy repos are protected from unauthorized modification. Common tags: policy-as-code, testing, review.
items.how_to_find: Confirm policies have test cases, changes go through review, and policy repos are protected from unauthorized modification.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Policies are unit-tested and reviewed" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — Policy as Code. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Policy coverage spans pipeline and runtime
items.description: The same intent is enforced both in CI (fast feedback) and at runtime (authoritative), avoiding gaps where one layer is the only control. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify key policies are enforced in both CI and admission/runtime, and that any single-layer gaps are intentional and risk-accepted. Common tags: policy-as-code, coverage, defense-in-depth.
items.how_to_find: Verify key policies are enforced in both CI and admission/runtime, and that any single-layer gaps are intentional and risk-accepted.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Policy coverage spans pipeline and runtime" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Infrastructure as Code — Policy as Code. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Security-relevant events are logged centrally
items.description: Authentication, authorization, and administrative actions are logged to a central, tamper-resistant store with sufficient retention. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm security events are centrally aggregated, logs are protected from tampering, and retention meets policy and compliance needs. Common tags: observability, logging, monitoring.
items.how_to_find: Confirm security events are centrally aggregated, logs are protected from tampering, and retention meets policy and compliance needs.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Security-relevant events are logged centrally" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Security Observability. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: OWASP Top Ten — Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

items.title: Actionable security alerts are configured
items.description: Detections for suspicious behavior (brute force, privilege escalation, anomalous egress) generate alerts routed to an owner with a response path. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify alert rules exist for key threat scenarios, route to an on-call owner, and are tuned to limit noise while catching real events. Common tags: observability, alerting, detection.
items.how_to_find: Verify alert rules exist for key threat scenarios, route to an on-call owner, and are tuned to limit noise while catching real events.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Actionable security alerts are configured" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Security Observability. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

references.title: OWASP Top Ten — Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

items.title: Runtime threat detection is deployed for workloads
items.description: Runtime sensors detect anomalous container/host behavior (unexpected process execution, file tampering, suspicious syscalls) in production. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm a runtime detection agent is deployed across workloads, rules cover common attack behaviors, and detections feed alerting. Common tags: observability, runtime, detection.
items.how_to_find: Confirm a runtime detection agent is deployed across workloads, rules cover common attack behaviors, and detections feed alerting.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Runtime threat detection is deployed for workloads" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Security Observability. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Configuration drift is detected and reconciled
items.description: Running infrastructure is continuously compared to its declared IaC state so manual or unauthorized changes are surfaced and reconciled. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify drift detection runs against production, surfaces out-of-band changes, and that drift is alerted on or auto-reconciled. Common tags: observability, drift, iac.
items.how_to_find: Verify drift detection runs against production, surfaces out-of-band changes, and that drift is alerted on or auto-reconciled.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Configuration drift is detected and reconciled" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Security Observability. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

sections.title: Runtime & Monitoring — Remediation SLAs & Feedback Loop

items.title: Vulnerability remediation SLAs are defined and tracked
items.description: Time-to-remediate targets are set by severity and tracked, so critical vulnerabilities are fixed within a bounded, measurable window. This is a high-impact finding that attackers frequently chain with other issues to escalate access or exfiltrate data. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm SLAs exist per severity, are tracked with dashboards, and that SLA breaches are escalated rather than silently aging. Common tags: feedback, sla, metrics.
items.how_to_find: Confirm SLAs exist per severity, are tracked with dashboards, and that SLA breaches are escalated rather than silently aging.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Vulnerability remediation SLAs are defined and tracked" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Remediation SLAs & Feedback Loop. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Production signals feed back into the pipeline
items.description: Incidents, runtime detections, and pentest findings generate new pipeline checks, threat-model updates, or guidance so the same class does not recur. While not always immediately exploitable, this weakness expands attack surface and should be remediated within the current sprint. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a mechanism converts recurring findings/incidents into new automated tests or controls, and that closure is tracked. Common tags: feedback, continuous-improvement, metrics.
items.how_to_find: Verify a mechanism converts recurring findings/incidents into new automated tests or controls, and that closure is tracked.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Production signals feed back into the pipeline" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Remediation SLAs & Feedback Loop. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: A vulnerability disclosure intake exists
items.description: External researchers have a clear, monitored channel to report vulnerabilities, and reports are triaged into the same remediation workflow. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Confirm a published disclosure policy/contact exists, reports are acknowledged within a target time, and feed the tracked SLA workflow. Common tags: feedback, disclosure, governance.
items.how_to_find: Confirm a published disclosure policy/contact exists, reports are acknowledged within a target time, and feed the tracked SLA workflow.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "A vulnerability disclosure intake exists" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Remediation SLAs & Feedback Loop. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.

items.title: Program metrics drive continuous improvement
items.description: Trend metrics (mean time to remediate, escape rate, coverage) are reviewed regularly to steer investment and demonstrate program maturity. This is a defense-in-depth gap that improves overall resilience when fixed. Assessors should confirm the control in practice, not only on paper: Verify a metric set is collected, reviewed on a cadence with stakeholders, and used to prioritize security program improvements. Common tags: feedback, metrics, maturity.
items.how_to_find: Verify a metric set is collected, reviewed on a cadence with stakeholders, and used to prioritize security program improvements.
items.how_to_fix: Remediate "Program metrics drive continuous improvement" by applying vendor and CIS/OWASP hardening guidance for Runtime & Monitoring — Remediation SLAs & Feedback Loop. Implement least privilege, enable required logging, validate in staging, then re-scan with the same tools. Track ownership and set a remediation date based on severity.
